response:
## Verdicts

| # | Finding | Verdict | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `route_metadata_drift` — `/settings/billing` (crime_00017) | **warn** (block if the PR touches any of these files) | Four competing names for one destination: path `billing`, component `PricingPage`, metadata.title `Plans`, `<title>` `Subscription`, nav labels `Plans` vs `Billing`. Real user-facing confusion (user clicks "Billing" in sidebar, lands on a tab titled "Subscription") plus agent confusion. Medium severity / 0.65 confidence is right — the drift exists, but the *canonical* name is a judgement call, so don't auto-block on main; do block any PR that edits one label without aligning the others. |
| 2 | `duplicated_navigation_source` — `/settings/billing` in `registry.ts` ("Plans") and `sidebar.ts` ("Billing") (crime_00015) | **warn** — fix in the same PR as #1 | Sub-symptom of #1 rather than an independent crime. Whoever picks the canonical name in #1 also collapses or documents one of these as the source of truth. Don't fix separately; it'll just re-drift. |
| 3 | `docs_code_drift` — `docs/teams.md:5 → ./setup.md` (crime_00018) | **warn** (low priority) | Single broken relative link, no runtime impact, severity `low`/confidence `0.9`. Easy fix, but blocking merges over one dead doc link is noise. Keep it visible so the next docs-touching PR sweeps it up, or it accumulates with the orphaned `/team` and `/workspace/members` routes the same scan flagged.|

## What I'd actually do

Treat #1 + #2 as **one fix**: pick the canonical destination name (the path is `/settings/billing`, so "Billing" reads honestly), then update `metadata.title`, the `<title>`, the component name, and collapse the two nav sources. That's a 15-minute edit and it removes both findings.

#3 stands alone but is cheap — fold it into the next docs PR rather than gating merges.

One thing to flag from the wider scan: the **`concept_alias_drift` finding (crime_00012)** sits adjacent to these IA findings and is the deeper cause — `docs/teams.md` calls the same concept "team" / "organisation" / "workspace" / "account" and there are orphaned routes for three of those names (crimes 26–28). If you fix the route/nav drift without picking a canonical tenant term, the drift will come back under different labels.
